desc:
A rocky protrusion has formed on the walls of the chasm, big enough to stand or
rest on with a small group, yet it is very uncomfortable. A few smaller bushes
grow on the edges, and some grass grows in the cracks of the wall. Every part of
the rock and bushes is damp, and small fungi grow all around. It seems to be
quite easy to climb down into the deep and dark chasm, but only a very skilled
climber would be able to find a way up without breaking his neck when trying.
name: A Protrusion in the Chasms Wall

desc:
The walls rise so high that only a small portion of the sky remains to be seen.
While some parts of the walls seem to offer some hold to plants and even smaller
trees, other parts are blank and steep, and not even a crack can be spotted. The
bottom of this chasm is moist, and large sections have been filled with dirty
water which stinks of decay and old mud. Some patches of black grass grow here,
and from the tenacious, black mud, a few broken bones of animals and travellers,
which have been so unlucky to slide into the chasm, stick out.
name: A Deep and Dark Chasm

desc:
Being this deep in the earth, this place seems more like a cave than a chasm.
Down here, it is dark and damp, and only a small glimpse of the sky can be seen
high above. A pool of dark, rotting water has formed underneath the northern
walls. The few plants and bushes that grow down here are blackened and so
twisted that it is not possible to tell from what kind their seeds have
originated. The only sign of rich vegetation is a mass of tendrils that hangs
down the eastern wall. In the dark mud, some marks have been left, running
towards the silent surface of the pool.
name: The Bottom of a Dark Chasm

desc:
The narrow walls of this cave are covered with white droppings. On the floor, a
thick carpet of these droppings combined with countless bones of mice or similar
creatures and deceased bats has formed. Everything on the floor seems to be wet
from trickling water. Many cracks can be seen in the high ceiling.
name: A Small Cave

desc:
The fields come to an abrupt end here, as the plateau is suddenly cut short by a
steep cliff to the south. The edge of the cliff is weather-beaten and cracked
from years of erosion, and grey lichen and green moss cover a few parts of the
scarp. Giant white rocks dot the flowery landscape below as a result of past
landslides. The grassy landscape below stretches out as far as the eye can see,
and to the south-west, a long ledge can be noticed.
name: On a Cliff

desc:
Up to the height of a large man, dense grass grows here in abundance. Fed by
frequent rainfall and the nutrient, dark brown soil below, the grass is strong
and sturdy, yielding great cover for all kinds of animals, making travelling in
this area rather tiresome. During all seasons except for winter, insects can be
seen and heard diligently buzzing around, and tiny beetles crawl on the thick
stems.
name: Field of High Grass

desc:
A green hillock almost seems to leap out of the middle of the fallow plains.
Although it only has the size of a full-grown oak tree, the existence of the
hillock is still remarkable on this otherwise flat landscape. Grass and
simbelmyne cover the slopes of the knoll, and broad bushes grow at its foot,
concealing the base of the hill with their branches and leaves.
name: On a Low Hill

desc:
A few small birch trees and a couple of oregano plants are all that break
through the sea of grey-brown grass that comprises the whole landscape. Small
animals, rain and erosion have dug holes in the ground, which are now covered
with grass, making travelling quite unpleasant in this area. Small surface
irregularities further worsen travel conditions, making riding impossible and
giving the grassy sea the appearance of having waves.
name: Sparse Grass

desc:
The cave is quite small. It smells of fresh and slightly moist soil. The
skeletons of two big animals can be seen; from their appearance, they could have
been bears. The bones are covered with notches, caused by swords or similar
weapons. Thousands of thin roots hang from the ceiling, and probably belong to
the vegetation growing above.
name: A Deserted Cave

desc:
At this small rise the view over the expanding plains is remarkable. Even though
the rise is but a few yards above the normal ground level, the lack of trees and
large bushes allows one to see almost a mile in each direction on a clear day.
Below the slope the grass stretches endlessly as far as the eye can see, only
broken by some bushes appearing in the north.
name: A Small Rise in the Barren Fields

desc:
The ground descends gently to the north, while the bushes appearing here and
there become more dense towards the bottom of the slope and finally form an
almost impenetrable thicket. The surrounding landscape is dominated by knee-deep
hay, especially in the east where no other noticeable features are in sight. A
network of vines crawls along the slope, climbing up a bush or a twig wherever
it has the chance to do so. Wide leaves of the vines add noticeable greenery to
the otherwise dull colour of this terrain.
name: Brush-covered Slope

desc:
Here, a thick and high hedgerow, yet no more than twenty feet wide, separates
the vast plains in the east from the grassland in the west. A dirt path winds in
the north-south direction inside the hedge, protected by a wall of bushes on
both sides. Even though peeking through the dense foliage is possible, finding a
way to pass through easily is very hard. To the north the hedges seem to be
growing closer towards each other, yet the path continues.
name: A Path through a Hedgerow

desc:
Hidden amidst a dense thicket of tall bushes, this small field is sheltered from
anything that could disturb the growth of its vegetation. The multitude of herbs
growing in the field and under the bushes suggests that a wide variety of plants
find just what they need here: a perfect combination of shade, nutrients and
water. Some of the plants seem uncommon and strange to these lands, almost as if
someone had planted them here, yet the amount of wild weeds indicates that no
one tends them.
name: A Patch of Herbs in the Thicket

desc:
A small spring with fresh and pure water beckons those who pass by to take a
refreshing drink. It has formed a small, clear puddle at the bottom of a sandy
hollow, where fragile plants draw nourishment through small, grey pebbles. The
grass around the spring grows low, yet there are no visible signs of animals
coming here to drink. Apart from the quiet shuffling of sand and leaves, there
usually seems to be a notable silence about this place, as if it was beckoning
the occasional traveller for rest and contemplation.
name: At a Clear Spring

desc:
The slightly sandy and rocky ground seems to have little to offer for the local
vegetation, yet some shrubs with crooked branches and dry needles grow here in
tightly packed groups. Occasional white and blood red stones add colour to the
dull grey soil; their shape is round and smooth, almost as if they had been
under running water in some ancient times. However, no signs of streams or
rivers can be seen amidst the shubbery dominating this rugged landscape.
name: Dry Shrubs

desc:
The branches of a huge maple tree hover above the small, round bushes growing
here. The tree seems very old, for many hollows filled with dry leaves and
broken twigs can be seen along its trunk. Judging from the signs of nesting in
the nooks and crannies, maples like this one seem to be quite popular among the
local bird life; the thin shrubs around it cannot offer such protection. The
brushwood continues in every direction, and seems to get thicker in the north
and west.
name: A Maple Tree among the Bushes

desc:
A still pond lies here in the middle of a dense forest, slowly being engulfed
into a growth of moss and reeds. The tiny leaves of some water plant form a
tinted green cover on its surface, suggesting that the water is very good for
their nourishment. The dark depths of the pond do not reveal anything from a
distance, while a shallow stream with a clearly visible bottom runs from the
pond towards the woods in the west. Some old snags stand out in the middle of
the pond, forming a tangle for birds and fishes to hide in.
name: A Green Pond in the Forest

desc:
Most of the trees in this part of the forest are old beeches with thick trunks
and reaching branches. Little light, be it sun or star or moon, can reach the
ground from the sky, yet dense undergrowth has spread over the mossy soil below.
Large boulders block some of the paths leading past the huge trees, especially
to the west where a cliff seems to rise from the deepest part of the forest.
name: Gloomy Forest

desc:
At this place, some paths coming from the surrounding brushes and trees meet,
while only low bushes grow nearby. The looming trees and their thick branches
with their many leaves and the thorny bushes prevent this place from being a
clearing, but compared to the rest of the forest, it is a place of free
movement. Yet this changes fast as one steps away from the meeting of the only
barely visible paths and gets closer to the high and dense shrubbery and the
thick stems of ancient and young trees.
name: Intertwined Paths in the Forest

desc:
Thick trees grow high towards the sky and block most of it. Below the huge and
darkening crowns, there do not grow many bushes, yet a thick entanglement of
tendrils and vines is interwoven with them. Only a few paths created by stronger
animals like boars lead through this very dense scrub, but they soon disappear
from sight between and below bushes or tendrils.
name: Intertwined Paths in the Forest

desc:
The dark waters of this somewhat overgrown pond do not allow one to see very far
beneath the surface. Quietly swaying water plants grow here amidst some
submerged trunks, getting shorter towards the deeper parts of the pond where
even less light is available. The muddy bottom of the pond seems soft and
treacherous as it is slowly sucking down broken branches and other litter. Near
the northern brim, some burly roots of living trees standing above the pond
reach into the murky waters. More immersed snags can be seen among these gnarled
roots.
name: Murky Waters of the Green Pond

desc:
The huge sunken tree above forms a dark ceiling to this small hollow far beneath
the surface. Some roots from the tall trees growing on the bank crawl towards
the bottom of the pond, and hold this tree firmly in their grasp. Dark green
weeds surround the hollow completely, but here, only occasional twigs and leaves
cover the dull, grey mud. Only one opening between the weeds and the roots seems
to lead towards more open waters.
name: Below a Sunken Tree

desc:
A small natural cave has formed over the past ages. The walls consist of hard
rock. The rain of past decades and centuries must have washed out all the soft
soil so that this cave has been able to take its form. The walls, ceiling and
floor are all covered with dozens of cracks of all sizes, yet there seems to be
not much circulation of fresh air and the temperature is stable. The floor is
covered with the tracks of snakes and mostly consists of little bones and dirt.
Seemingly thousands of small animals like rabbits, mice, rats and maybe even
foxes must have found their final demise down here. A part of the eastern wall
looks as if it had collapsed some time ago.
name: The Cave of Snakes

desc:
Cracks run along the walls and the ceiling of this small natural cave. The
bottom is not only covered with the remains of many little animals but also with
many skins of snakes that outgrew them. Near the lower part of the cave, most of
the cracks become broader. The air is dry and smells of earth and something else
strongly. Near the walls, many nests of snakes with dozens of eggs can be seen.
name: Nest of the Snakes

desc:
A narrow dirt path, separating from the trail to the west, follows the border of
a dense forest here. Tall trees rise above the path in the south, screening it
with their reaching branches. Only some roots lie on the path, while tangled
undergrowth and fallen trunks seem to dominate the parts deeper in the forest.
Smooth grassland spreads out to the east past the wooded area.
name: Under the Eaves of a Dense Forest

desc:
The occasional grinding squeak of smooth, water-worn stones can be heard while
travelling along this path. The dirt here is hard-packed, indicating that at one
stage, this was a frequently used trail. The dimmed roaring sounds of a great
river can be heard from the northwest.
name: A Pebble-strewn Trail

desc:
Between a dark oak forest to the north and the mighty Anduin river to the south
and east stretches a green meadowland. The grass grows tall. Groups of young
trees provide some shelter. The riverbank to the south is steep; most animals
seem to go east to drink out of the river. This pasture looks like a good
hunting ground, and a rich tent to the west could belong to a hunter.
name: Plains between a Forest and the River

desc:
These tall trees, standing shoulder to shoulder like green sentinels, bent by
the prevailing winds, seem to bow down in deference to the awesome might of the
river. Their mossy, mottled branches droop down occasionally to caress its
waters and they offer a small refuge of shade for the hot, tired traveller.
name: Small Shady Grove

desc:
The slopes of the knoll are sprinkled with flora of all kinds. The lush soil
here shows hints of life everywhere. Small animals made their homes into these
hillocks, as little holes can be seen which were dug into the ground. These
fertile lands continue towards the north. A weak trail circulates around this
higher area, approaching from the east and leaving to the south-west.
name: The Flourishing Knoll

desc:
The trail slithers through the plains and stretches from the west to the east.
At this point, the course of this trail circulates around a low knoll,
protecting the traveller from the winds of the open plains. At times, the path
weakens and almost merges with the landscape. Tiny little pebbles cover the
ground together with several boulders, most of them already partially covered
with grass.
name: A Pebble-strewn Trail across the Plains

desc:
The trail stretches here from the west to the north, where it suddenly makes a
sharp bend, disappearing out of view. Some low bushes grow on the path, forcing
the traveller to go around them. Some tall cherry bushes flourish to the east,
offering a sheltered place for a rest.
name: A Bend in the Trail

desc:
A meandering trail travels through the lush area here. The grass covering the
sides of the narrow, muddy path is almost enshrouding the pathway from sight at
some places. Slightly sloping plains border the trail to the north and the west,
while the trail itself continues towards the east and the south.
name: A Bend in the Trail

desc:
A great number of low, grass-covered hillocks irregularly rises from the ground
like soft waves on the ocean. Some small tufts of various flowers and herbs can
be found around here. Lush, green fields continue towards all directions,
offering no visible change to the landscape.
name: The Undulating Fields

desc:
Lush grounds surround this area with an undying growth of florets, while fertile
grass and a sea of various herbs colour the landscape here. To the west, the
great river of Anduin rushes on its travelling course. A small tree sits on the
bank of the great Anduin, bathed in the moisture of the river. A fertile plain
seems to continue in the distance to the east and the south.
name: Fertile Plains

desc:
Various bushes grow here, being well-nurtured by the moisture of the river.
Occasionally, leaves fall into the rippling stream, only to be carried away by
the strong current. Some bare rock can be seen wherever the flooding waters have
washed away the soil. Further away from the riverbed, strong, tall grass
dominates the landscape.
name: Bank of the River Anduin

desc:
A translucent spring is welling up in the middle of the field, offering a good
place to drink for various wild animals. Low bushes form an arch around it, as
if trying to hide it with their evergreen leaves. Wild flowers grow here in
abundance, adding colour to the scene. Thick grass covers the plains as far as
the eye can see.
name: The Translucent Spring

desc:
Dense, purple bushes of lavender cover the plains here, making it hard to travel
through them. Small hummocks of dark green grass try to make their way to free
air but are choked by the density of the bushes. More low bushes can be seen to
the south, colouring the green plain with darker spots. The great river of
Anduin runs by to the north and the west, increasing the humidity here.
name: The Lavender Bush

desc:
The ground here is uneven from tussocks of thick, dark grass. Some small stones
lie around, mostly buried under soil. From among the tussocks, a couple of
flowers are striving on their way to open air. A gentle rise in the landscape
offers an opportunity to eye the surroundings. A higher spot to the north seems
to have a building on it, while endless plains continue towards all other
directions.
name: The Bumpy Plains

desc:
A low, wooden shelter was built here, now partially collapsed and rotten. Its
walls are made from thin, soft twigs which are tied together with small branches
cut from the nearby bushes. The partially decayed structure can still offer good
protection from what falls from the sky. The stakes of the shelter were made of
soft wood which rot and time have partially destroyed already. A wide circle of
stones is located in front of the shelter, still having some charred wood on it.
Vast plains open to the east and the south, and a steep riverbank descends to
the river Anduin to the west.
name: Old Campsite

desc:
A thick and tall layer of grass sways here constantly, forming a perfect green
sea. The ground is a dry mixture of soil and sand, yet offering a fertile base
for the grassy vegetation. No other growth is visible here, but some faint
trails of wild animals can be spotted around. Green plains continue onwards in
all directions from here.
name: The Lush Steppe

desc:
The dry soil here makes it impossible for anything to thrive. The broken and
folded land extends to all directions. Some small tussocks of grey and yellow
grass struggle here among a couple of sharp stones, partly buried deep under the
hard surface. The ground seems to turn more fertile to all directions.
name: The Desolate Depression

desc:
The ground here is fairly even, moist soiled, darkish red, and quite lush. The
grass covering it is short but strong. Some low, rust-hued bushes grow here in
quite irregular forms, adding colour to the scenery. Eastwards, the ground
becomes more arid, offering no good base for any growth there. Green fields open
to all directions on the slightly undulating plains.
name: Rust-hued Bushes

desc:
These arid plains do not have much to offer. The earth is brown and cracked by
the dry air. Old footprints have dried into the ground, showing vague signs of
life. Yellow and green vegetation stands out in contrast to the dry land. To the
south, an odd shape in the scenery distinguishes itself from the surroundings,
while the dry landscape continues towards the east.
name: Arid Plains

desc:
The dirt which forms the trail is barely visible below the tall grass. The path
meanders from the east to the west, narrowing down and almost disappearing at
times. Tall grass, shrubs, and various low bushes cover the area to the north,
while a low grove of cherry bushes can be found nearby to the south.
name: Overgrown Trail

desc:
A small grove has formed to a small depression in the ground here. Cherry shrubs
grow all around, forming a nice shelter from wind and rain. Yellow and blue
flowers grow on the rich ground. Fertile growth continues towards the east,
while the gently sloping surface to the west and the north remains a green sea
of grass. To the west, a vague trail slithers through the plains, turning to the
north close by.
name: The Cherry Grove

desc:
The grass here is very weak and short, making some dense bushes stand out. These
short bushes add a diverse set of colours to the plains here. Some faint paths
of animals can be seen between the hedges. A small nest is built into the edge
of one shrub. The slightly bushy area continues for a while towards the north,
while the landscape to the east rises slightly, only to form an odd round shape.
name: Dense Bushes

desc:
A few, low hillocks rise from the ground here, forming a low, circular valley.
In the middle of the depression, a lonely, small apple tree stands as a gracious
opposite to the never-ending plains all around it. Tall grass around the tree
strongly stretches upwards, almost hiding a small spring welling up nearby.
name: Grassy Hillocks

desc:
Low bushes grow here, partially covering the small pathway. The places with tall
grass and the weak growth of trees make it sometimes impossible to tell where
the way runs. Above the plants, hilly fields can be seen stretching out to all
directions. The path opens up to the east as the bushes become thinner.
name: Overgrown Trail

desc:
The top of this knoll offers a view over the surrounding plains. No other
similar formations can be seen nearby. The surface is covered by thick grass,
and a few small trees grow here, stretching their branches all around them. To
the west, the vegetation thickens in the bushy area, while the weak form of a
trail can be seen to the east, curving through the flat plains. A strange, quite
rotten half of a large trunk lies on ground here.
name: A Lone Hillock

desc:
Low tunnels were dug into the side of a hillock. The walls are made of dirt, and
the floor is covered with ankle-deep mud. The narrow tunnel continues towards
the north, fading into darkness there. Some thin roots of vegetation above hang
from the roof and the walls of the cavern. A few small bugs run around
everywhere, making the walls seem to be alive.
name: A Filthy Tunnel

desc:
The narrow tunnel widens here. Sturdy supports line the tunnel, holding up the
wooden beams and preventing it from collapsing. Fresh footprints cover the dry,
smooth ground. A steep bend in the passage turns to the east, where a mild
source of light can be more sensed than seen. Small bugs and spiders run around
everywhere, making the cavern floor crawl.
name: Dark Tunnel

desc:
The room is lit by one small torch on the wall. Dirt, filthy rags and garbage
cover the floor here, while a strong stench of rotten food and mould fills the
air. A wide crack from the floor to the ceiling is visible in the eastern wall.
Some disgusting pieces of waste lie on ground, having strong marks of teeth on
them. To the west, the light dims to total darkness.
name: Squalid Cavern

desc:
The flourishing lea here is filled with a wide range of different growth. Thick,
strong grass covers the ground with its vital, green shade. A large selection of
different herbs also grows here, adding colour to the flat ground with their
blossoms. Smooth plains continue towards all directions from here.
name: A Blooming Field

desc:
Smooth, constantly swirling, tall grass creates the image of a wide area of
green water here. The flat area turns into gently sloping plains to the east,
while the growth thins out on the field to the west and turns into dry and
broken lands. The form of an unusually tall tree decorates the view to the
south.
name: A Flat Sea of Grass

desc:
The low plains here are extraordinarily lush. Small tussocks cover the ground,
making it hard to travel on it. A few shrubs struggle to gain in height under an
unusually tall tree standing here. The top of the tree reaches towards the sky,
while it stretches out its ramified branches. A faint trail slithers through the
plains to the south.
name: A Lone Tree

desc:
The brown, dusty path is wide and smooth at some spots, while it can barely be
espied at some places below all the overgrowing plants. Tall grass also borders
those clean parts of the trail, threatening to bury it completely. The trail
turns from the east to the south here, while a lone hillock with bushy slopes
rises to the west, blocking easy access there.
name: A Rocky Trail

desc:
Small bushes follow the path on both sides from the west to the north, partially
hiding it below their growth. Dry, stony fields stretch all around this rather
flat land, only having a few stunted bushes and dying grass growing on it. The
trail continues towards the north where it curves eastwards through the plains.
name: A Rocky Trail Bend

desc:
Small brown pebble covers the flat land here. Some grass growth has made its way
through the rough soil, breaking the surface and allowing a few small bushes to
root here. The grass grows stronger to the east and the west, where the ground
gently rises. The dry area to the north is covered with tall but weak grass and
several small bushes.
name: The Pebbly Plains

desc:
The grass here is short and shifts in a range of yellow-brownish colours. The
meagre vegetation is the result of the dry and thin soil. No trees grow within
sight. The scenery slightly slopes towards a lower region north-west of here.
The signs of wildlife are sparse, only some faint tracks on the ground imply
that this vast grassland is not completely lifeless.
name: A Grassy Slope

desc:
The terrain slightly descends downwards to the west from here, but otherwise it
lacks any outstanding features. The area, devoid of hills and ridges, was once
flat land which seems to have been reshaped by the movement of water. The
vegetation in this area mainly consists of waist-high grass, which gets its much
needed moisture only from the occasional rainfalls.
name: A Small Slope

desc:
As the trail makes a turn between the north and the west, it traverses the slope
between the western lowlands and the higher land to the east. Despite the slope,
the trampled earth still provides pretty good footing. A few bushes try to cover
and hide the pebbly path here. Just close to the east, a large stone structure
stands out against the grass-covered plains.
name: A Sloping Trail

desc:
The trail bends to the south and the east here, as if it was avoiding the cairn
to the south-east. The trail is nothing more than a faint track in the dirt here
with yellowish, dry grass growing several feet high on its sides. A lone figure
of a large tree can be seen nearby to the west as a sole landmark on these
unending fields of grass.
name: A Bend in the Trail

desc:
The ground to the west slightly slopes downwards. What once was marshes and
lakes has been turned into a flatland by now, vegetated by dry, yellow grass and
patches of weed which have found their footing in the poor soil. To the north
and the west, a wide plain stretches out, covered with yellow grass. South of
here, a small, winding trail is barely visible between grass, weeds and low
bushes.
name: Sloping Grassland

desc:
These gently sloping plains are covered with a thick, bold greenish layer of
various grasses. No other growth can be found around. A few weak tracks can be
seen where the grass has been walked on, giving a hint of rich wildlife around.
The landscape continues towards all directions as a similar, slightly sloping
plain.
name: Blooming Grass

desc:
Some faded remains of a path are still visible here. A few, partially overgrown
and flat stones have been buried in the ground, once forming an inviting
pathway. Now, a thick layer of grass and moss covers most of the stepping
stones. Small piles of rocks can be seen nearby, mostly buried under tall weeds
and low bushes. Flat, grassy plains open to the south and the west.
name: Part of a Pathway

desc:
The even land continues towards all directions, not showing any distinguishing
features to break off against the monotonous terrain. Knee-high grass covers the
ground, shifting in different colours. Occasionally, some spots of taller weeds
can be found scattered in the surroundings.
name: Flat Grassland

desc:
The trail stretches to the east and the west through the lush grassland. As the
terrain is fairly even and lacks any natural obstacles, the trail can maintain
its course fairly straight. At some places, the trail is merely a small, dirty
track, mostly trampled by animals and almost overgrown by grass and weed at
parts. To the south from here, a large stone structure forms a distinguishable
landmark. An almost completely overgrown path branches off from the trail,
leading south towards the structure.
name: Trail by a Cairn

desc:
A large rock pile dominates the area. It stands out against the grass in
solitude, being visible from several miles away in these flatlands. The cairn
rises high from the ground level and looks as if it were climbable. A weak,
dirty track meanders from the passing trail just to the north of here, ending in
the front of a narrow opening between two large stone blocks. The end of the
hollow opening fades into darkness inside the cairn.
name: At an Old Cairn

desc:
The chamber inside the cairn is large enough to provide room for a few persons.
The floor consists of trampled earth, and the solid stone walls are covered with
moss and lichen. Earth and stones make up a waterproof ceiling, making this
place a good shelter from forces of nature. The ground is littered with old,
mostly broken equipment left behind by earlier visitors.
name: Inside the Cairn

desc:
The cairn is skilfully built of stones and rocks of different sizes, making it
easy to find a footing on the flat top of the structure. The top is a few feet
wide and steeply descends to all other directions but the north. The elevated
position gives an impressive view over the slightly undulating plain around.
name: The Top of the Cairn

desc:
Dry grass unfolds in all directions along this flat highland section. The grass
grows to the height of a foot and covers most of the ground together with some
scattered patches of taller weeds. The plains continue slightly descending
towards the east and the west.
name: Dried Grass

desc:
Short, dry grass and hummocks of weeds make up the majority of the vegetation in
the area. The ground is poor, and the soil dusty. The landscape descends
slightly to the west. An enormous pile of various sized rocks blocks the way to
the north. A more open and fertile field opens to the east, while the area to
the south remains dry.
name: Dry Grassland

desc:
The terrain here sinks into a vast, natural bowl, looking very much like a
dried-out lake or an old watering hole. In the middle of the depression, there
grow tall, thick grass and green-leaved bushes. Unlike the meagre vegetation of
the surrounding area, the grass here looks green and healthy. Several weak
tracks of wild animals mark the soft ground, only to disappear into the small
holes dug in the slopes.
name: A Slight Depression

desc:
The dry, barren landscape dominates the scenery here, only broken off by some
scattered weeds and a few thorny bushes resembling small islands in the yellow
sea of grass. The thorny bushes are small and gnarled, resulting from many years
of influence of rain and wind. The ground slightly descends to the west, and the
surface there seems to be more fertile.
name: Sparse Grassland

desc:
Vast, grass covered plains continue as far as the eye can see. The terrain is
fairly even without any drastic elevation changes, making the view pretty dull,
but allowing one to travel over the fields rather effortlessly. Whenever the
winds blow, the grass shifts in wave-like patterns across the treeless fields.
name: Grassy Fields

desc:
Only the yellow waist-high grass grows here on the dry ground. The soil is
porous and sandy, allowing the water of occasional rainfalls to be absorbed
quickly. A few fresh holes in the ground indicate that this area is not entirely
devoid of animal life. Even fields open to every direction, remaining flat and
dull.
name: Grass-covered Fields

desc:
The treeless plain unfolds like a grassy blanket until it is lost in the
distance. There is nothing else but the flat sea of grass here, which covers the
ground with a dense layer. The vegetation growing on these plains depends on the
amount of moisture which can be retained in the soil. When judging from the
yellow-brownish colours, the land is dry, and rain is seldom in these lands.
name: Vast Grassland

desc:
The plains here are covered with yellowish grass, growing waist-high from the
dry and sandy ground. As the soil is too dry and infertile to support any larger
growth, only a small selection of weeds populate the fields. Despite the barren
appearance, some small holes in the ground as well as a few scattered tracks
reveal that some wildlife can be able to endure in this area.
name: Grass-covered Plains

desc:
The flat terrain here is mainly covered with yellow, waist-high grass, mixed
with tall patches of higher-grown weeds. As an occasional breeze sweeps over the
land, the grass seems to dance along with the gusts of wind. A large, round
structure to the west is the only apparent sign of habitation in the area. The
never ending grassland stretches out to all other directions.
name: Flat Grassland

desc:
As weather-worn and rugged, these fields give a very barren impression, mostly
because of the dry, yellow grass which makes up the main vegetation here. The
flat terrain does not provide any natural landmarks except a small, winding
trail which passes by to the north. To the south, the east and the west, these
flat lands continue towards the horizon.
name: Barren Fields

desc:
The flat ground is covered by dry, yellow grass growing no more than a couple of
feet high. The ground seems to be arid in this area. The much needed water
resources only come from occasional rainfalls. Some scattered animal droppings
on the ground reveal that the area is not completely deserted, despite the
seemingly inhospitable environment. To the north, a faint trail is barely
discernible between the weeds.
name: Grassy Plains

desc:
A small, winding trail meanders across the plains in a straight east-west
course. The trail does not seem to be used frequently, as patches of weed have
sprung up on the trampled path. At times, the trail is barely discernible as it
loses itself in the waist-high yellow grass. The vast grassland which surrounds
the trail looks rough and desolate.
name: A Faint Trail

desc:
The ground is almost entirely covered with dry grass and tall weeds. The terrain
is flat and lacks any distinguishing features. The dry grass reaches no more
than a couple of feet from the ground and slightly shifts whenever a gust of
wind touches it. Some scattered animal droppings and tracks reveal recent
activity of wildlife.
name: Grassy Fields

desc:
The ground here is mostly even, without any discernible forms of hills or
ridges. The surface is dry and sandy and is not able to support more growth than
this of weak grass and a few patches of weed which grow in the area. To the
south, a weak trail can be seen running from the east to the west. The flat
terrain stretches out to all directions, leaving the landscape without any
noteworthy features.
name: Grassland beside a Trail

desc:
The trail continues towards the east and the west, twisting and turning between
patches of yellowish grass on its course through the wide grasslands. At its
wider sections, the trail is easy to travel on and wide enough for a small
wagon, but occasionally, it narrows down to be no more than a small track in the
dirt, and it is almost impossible to follow it. The growth bordering the path is
trodden down at places, as some animals have been trudging their way through the
grass.
name: A Winding Trail

desc:
The path here runs meandering to the east and the west through the grass-
covered fields. The slight depression in the ground following the path suggests
that this trail has been quite well used in the past, although it seems long
abandoned now. On both sides of the trail, the grassland stretches out as far as
the eye can see. To the west of here, a round stone structure can be seen in a
close distance.
name: A Faint Trail

desc:
The yellow, dry grass and weeds which cover the majority of the ground here grow
no higher than a couple of feet. The flat terrain was once a moist and
waterlogged grassland but has now dried out. The scenery consists of vast
meadows now, stretching out to all directions. To the south, a meandering trail
traverses through the flatland in a straight east-west course.
name: Grass beside a Trail

desc:
On the ground, there lie some scattered chunks of an old headstone, shattered
into pieces and mostly buried under soil or overgrown with moss and tall
grasses. Some small cairns are piled around it, barely visible through the thick
layer of vegetation. One large boulder rests on a few smaller stones. Parts of a
collapsed stone fence can still be found around this place.
name: Open Fields

desc:
The lush ground blooms with an undying growth of florets, as some fertile herbs
and tall grass colour the scenery. The ground under the vegetation is somewhat
soft and humid. Widely open plains seem to continue towards the distances
towards all directions from here, except to the north, where some unnatural form
in the scenery can be seen.
name: A Blooming Field

desc:
An ancient, well-made chamber used for burial purposes is carved to stone here.
Everything is covered with thick dust, and cobwebs hang in every corner. In the
middle of the room, there is a wide stone table, on which an ancient sarcophagus
lies. It has some barely noticeable, engraved letters on its side. Otherwise,
the room is free of furniture.
name: Burial Mound

desc:
A few struggling, thorny bushes lie here, fighting for their existence. The
barren ground barely allows them to sink their roots into it. Some tussocks of
yellow and brown grass is visible around. The landscape around is flat, and
lacks all noticeable features.
name: Stunted Growth

desc:
This was once a secure place with heavy stone walls protecting the entrance to a
small village. Now, the walls are mostly collapsed, and on the remaining parts,
there grow moss and small trees. A few ancient, mostly ruined buildings are
located south of here, forming blackened ghosts in this once harmonious place.
Weak remains of a pathway leave to the north through a collapsed archway.
name: Collapsed Fortification

desc:
This once enormous, welcoming building has been badly damaged over time. Lower
parts of the walls are made of large boulders, and the higher part, or whatever
is left of it, was made of huge logs. Now, the wood has mostly decayed, and
stones on walls are blackened from fires blazing here in the past. A few rotten
roof rafters lie on ground, overgrown by lichen.
name: A Roofless Building

desc:
A depressing, chewed up central square is here, connecting a few more or less
ruined buildings around it and a massive stone wall to the east. Small trees
have broken the paved ground, lifting the cobblestones from their holes. An
empty, dry fountain is here in the middle of the depredated area. To the north,
the back of a building is badly blackened by fires in the past.
name: The Inner Court

desc:
The remains of a large bed are here in one corner, completely decomposed but yet
recognisable from its shape, even though it is hard to tell what is the bed, and
what is the floor there. A small window in the stone wall is long broken,
resulting in shards of glass lying on the floor. Some large holes in the roof
reveal the decaying process going on here.
name: A Small House

desc:
This small building, consisting of one large room, shows strange marks of
habitation. A few musty duvets lie in a corner, still free of signs of decay.
The roof of this building seems to be intact, protecting the room from the
changes of weather. The hastily built door to the north is set up with a large
bar which can be used to lock the door from inside. A sturdy obstacle to the
south blocks the way through the narrow opening in the wall.
name: A Small House

desc:
The great river turns from the north to the west here, while the strong current
causes some backwaters to form. The riverbanks to the south and the east are
high and steep, making the riverbed run through a low pass between plains. A few
young willows and alders grow on the banks, only waiting for the next season of
floods to wash them away.
name: Pass in the Anduin

desc:
The river Anduin makes a bend here, flowing from the east to the south. Some
small rocks and larger boulders cover the riverbanks. Some litter is alternately
appearing and disappearing in the backwaters which the current forms at a few
spots. Open, grassy plains with only some bushes growing on them open behind the
riverbanks to the north and the west. A kind of a large forest darkens the
horizon in the north-east.
name: The River Anduin

desc:
Leaves and other litter slowly float down the strongly flowing stream, fallen
down into the river somewhere in the distance. A single, large boulder stands in
the middle of the river, splitting it in two. To the south, a few trees hover
above the riverbank, while the area to the east is covered with some low bushes.
name: The River Anduin

desc:
Conflicting currents produce odd pockets of calmness and furious churning
everywhere else. Enormous boulders here and there within the waters add to this
effect. Around them, the water splashes violently up into the air before
rejoining the flow. The tumultuous waters of the Anduin surge southwards.
name: Bend in the Anduin

desc:
This powerful river continues onwards, inexorably. The waters are muddy brown
and thick with soil, loose reeds, driftwood and branches that it has collected
from riverbanks on its tremendous journey. The fast current carries the dirt
southwards. Around some stones that are too big to be washed away the current
forms small foaming waves.
name: The Great River Anduin

desc:
The Anduin River's course is diverted around a section of land to the south,
where a cliff blocks its path and is battered at futilely by the raging,
indignant waters. Above stands a grove of trees, their branches drooping low
over the face of the small cliff, sometimes touching the waters that spray up.
In the right season, they even reach the surface waters, when the river is
higher and filled by the rains of pregnant clouds.
name: Bend in the Anduin by a Cliff

desc:
This tiny chamber was created to an excavation inside the cairn. The cleverly
hidden entrance shows that this room was built with the intention to keep it
concealed. The ceiling arches low, forming a durable roof for this room. Some
old, musty supply items are still lying here, waiting for decay to destroy them.
name: A Hidden Storage Chamber

desc:
Colossal stalagmites stand like guardians while stalactites loom ominously
overhead, lending a distinct feeling of being within the jaws of some
prodigious, sinister creature. Scintillating crystal formations sparkle when
light catches them, bringing the ordinary rocks to life - like flowers do in
grassy fields. In the middle of the cave is a rude camp, just some dirty mats
around an old fireplace.
name: Subterranean Cavern

desc:
Fluorescent moss on the walls emits a faint light, bathing the tunnel with an
eerie green glow. This illuminated passage winds and eventually begins to curve
sharply northwards, leading into a large cavern. A freshwater spring is here,
trickling and collecting where the water has eroded a small basin in the rock.
Innumerable bones litter the floor near the cavern's entrance.
name: Subterranean Tunnel

desc:
The cave has numerous small, dark ponds that were probably filled by water
finding its way in during times when the river swelled. Some of the walls are a
little moist, encouraging growths of moss. Water drips from the ceiling and
echoes across the cave. This, combined with the muted cacophony of the river
pounding against the cliffs, produces a watery orchestra.
name: Underground Entrance

desc:
It is hard to tell if the surroundings consist of murky water or dense mud and
bog; it is probably something in between. In some places the water is so liquid
that you are forced to swim. The deeper you dive down, the more liquid the mud
seems to become.
name: Deep, Murky Mud

desc:
Down here, it is a bit easier to swim than higher above, but the surroundings
still consist of a very tenacious foul liquid. Soil, old roots, rotten grasses,
and other undefined parts of mud and bog make up this swamphole, in which you
can more feel than see your surroundings.
name: Bottom of a Deep Swamphole

desc:
The water and mud around are too dense to see anything at all. You are lost and
cannot find your way back, even knowing what is up and what is down is a very
hard task in this dense, foul bog. The only chance seems swimming towards what
might be upwards - and hope.
name: Lost in a Deep Swamphole

desc:
The water does not seem that deep here but the ground consists of very soft mud
and bog, so actually standing or walking on it is quite hard. The ceiling
becomes somewhat higher and is covered completely in hanging, black roots.
Everything around, even the soil itself, seems to exude a strong smell of decay.
name: Dark Water

desc:
Very dark, muddy water has collected here and fills the tunnel to a larger part.
From all around thin nets of roots hang into the water, while the soil in the
walls feels very soft. So soft that a collapse of entire portions of them does
not seem that unlikely.
name: Dark Water

desc:
The dark, putrid water seems to lie under the cover of dark whisps of cold mist.
The same mist seems to hang everywhere: below the ceiling covered by roots, as
well as around the soft, wet walls of the dark and stinking soil. Drops of dirty
water constantly drop from the tips of roots and splash into the surface of the
still, stinking water, like drops of corrupted rain.
name: Dark Water in a Dripping Tunnel

desc:
The area is much drier here than to the west, yet the soil is still quite wet.
Countless fungi have started to grow in the dark down here and cover the walls
as well as the floor. Most of these fungi are white but others are also pitch
black. Here and there, a few dead roots hang into the tunnel and provide a
ground for the patches of small, white fungi with a unusually pointed hat.
name: A Stinking Tunnel Overgrown with Fungi

desc:
The air is still filled with foulness and around are still patches of fungi,
rotting roots, and corrupted soil, but there is also a hint of fresher air. The
area here ascends quite steeply from the dark water in the west and becomes a
very easy climb at the narrow cavern's end towards the east.
name: Steeply Rising Ground

desc:
What first seems to be a pond turns out to be mostly shallow, a few deeper holes
that left aside. The entire pond smells of rot and decay and once in a while a
bubble of gas breaks open on the black surface. Decaying things of all kinds are
rotting in the corrupted water and fill the air of this cave with their choking
stench.
name: A Foul, Shallow Pond of Mud

desc:
The water here is very foul and rotten. It stinks and is more mud than water and
you have a feeling as if the mud or unseen hands would try to pull you down. The
rather liquid mud seems to become even fouler and much more treacherous to the
east.
name: Very Foul, Stinking Water

desc:
The mud has a deadly drag downwards and it even feels as if some invisible hands
were grabbing your feet, pulling you downwards. The extremely foul air makes you
choke and shortly after your mouth fills with the stinking mud, your lungs
follow, and you suffocate.
name: Deadly Mud

desc:
The air is cold in this tunnel, but all the shimmering ice that has formed along
the walls seems not to belong here. The dirty crystals of frozen moisture,
growing only on the wall near the tunnel's western end, should have melted, as
the tunnel towards the east is still too warm for ice to form. Something towards
the west must make them persist.
name: A Narrow, Ice-covered Tunnel

desc:
This tunnel cuts through the wet soil below the plains. The entire area stinks
of the mud above, and everything is dripping wet. The walls and floor are wet
and decaying, and countless roots of plants from the plain hang from above and
keep touching you. A somewhat strange feeling also fills this place, yet it is
hard to exactly say what it is, but it surely feels a bit colder than it should
here.
name: A Narrow, Muddy Tunnel

desc:
The ever-present stink of decay and corruption here is much stronger than in any
other area around and even seems to become corporal and actually feelable. The
roots and mud around are much fouler than they naturally could be and small
clouds of mist hang over the floor and seem to creep into the roots all around.
name: The Heart of Corruption

desc:
All around is only darkness and the temperature is freezing cold. The air and
floor do not feel like substance at all, yet what you actually breathe and stand
on is impossible to tell.
name: Lost in the Freezing Dark

desc:
A pungent smell emanates from this cavern. Two branches lie against the northern
wall, they have been chewed upon and are encircled by twigs, moss and rotten
leaves, which cover the entire cavern floor. The walls bear many large claw
marks, going as deep through the hard-packed earth as through the thick rock.
name: Dark Cave

desc:
The ground is littered with chipped wood and small bones, having come from the
tree logs and animal carcasses that are tended to in this yard. The carcasses
lie to the north, where they are to be skinned and boned. The logs lie to the
west, an axe resting in one of them. A foul odour comes from the east, where a
massive pile of garbage awaits.
name: Labour Yard

desc:
Crates, shelves, and cupboards are placed strategically around the storehouse,
leaving a very narrow path between them. A great deal of chopped wood has been
stacked against the northern wall. A few crates have been broken open, yet
appear to be empty.
name: Storage Room

desc:
Piles of dirty pots and kettles are stacked dangerously high on the corner
counter of the kitchen. A tub with dirty water filled with more dirty pots sits
alongside another pile of poorly cleaned plates. A large hearth stands to the
south with a stew-filled kettle boiling underneath the fire. Various cooking
instruments and foodstuffs are placed strategically on the kitchen tables.
Hanging off a nail on one of the tables is a pail, half-full with trash.
name: Crowded Kitchen

desc:
Three rickety tables and matching benches fill the mess hall. The tables are
quite different in colour, as much from food stains of the past as from strongly
decayed pieces of food that stick between the wooden boards. Empty pots of stew
lie cluttered on the ground, a few of them accompanied with some stale content.
The aroma of strong liquor dominates the hall and absorbs all other scents.
name: Mess Hall

desc:
Short blades of grass that grow in the training yard are covered with dried out
blood, giving the grass a reddish brown colour. Most of the grass has been
trampled, the footprints of armoured feet clearly visible. Wooden bunkers have
been erected on the eastern and western side, whilst the smell of warm food
comes from the north. The training yard continues to the south.
name: Training Yard

desc:
A great deal of broken weapons lie abandoned against the wall of the warg pens,
that lie to the west. The smell of sweat pervades the training yard. Many
different contraptions are scattered in the yard, such as straw puppets, some
which are beheaded and no longer resemble a humanoid form at all. The training
yard continues to the north.
name: Training Yard

desc:
Many beds have been stacked on top of each other, and together with some wooden
ladders form a few dozen bunkbeds. The beds are made of straw, a woolen blanket
can be found on a few beds. Some of the blankets are badly torn, and have some
blood on them. A few chamber pots stand next to the beds, most of them empty. To
the south, more bunkbeds can be seen.
name: Sleeping Quarters

desc:
Many beds have been stacked on top of each other, forming bunkbeds. Wooden
ladders have been put against them, to allow easy climbing onto them. The beds
themselves are made of a light coloured wood, with brown straw upon it. One
bunkbed in particular catches the eye, as one of its beds is broken, having been
cracked in half in the middle. The south bears many more bunkbeds.
name: Sleeping Quarters

desc:
A few crates have been positioned in the centre of the sleeping quarters,
surrounded by many bunkbeds. The crates are mostly nailed down, save for some.
The walls of the hut are made of dark wood, filthied somehow, as the wood is
deeply discoloured in various places. More bunkbeds lie to the north.
name: Sleeping Quarters

desc:
A pile of bones of varying sizes rests against the southern wall. Pieces of bone
also litter the area, from mere chips to being chewed in half. Manure has been
assembled near the gate of the pen to the east, the odour of it permeating the
air. Fresh excrements lie scattered around, large enough to be easily avoided.
name: Warg Pen

desc:
A great deal of straw-filled bunkbeds are strategically placed in the hut, most
less than three feet apart. One bed against the eastern wall is covered in
blood, although it has mostly dried up already. A small window has been placed
in the western wall of this hut, thick bars visible on the other side. The
sleeping quarters continue to the north.
name: Sleeping Quarters

desc:
Thick logs, sharp and formed like cylinders at the edges, are tied together with
thick brown vines and form a palisade to the south. A latched wooden gate is
visible, laden with knife- and axe-sized cuts. The ground is hard brown dirt,
with a strong odour rising from a few darker spots. A bucket stands in the
northeastern corner, filled with a pile of animal bones, stripped entirely of
their meat. A rickety wooden ladder stands against the northern wall, leading
upwards through a small trapdoor.
name: Hidden Orkish Outpost

desc:
The cave is about thirty feet high here, and there are a lot of rocks and
boulders in between moon-pale stalagmites on the floor. The smell is very strong
here. There are carcasses of animals and of unrecognisable creatures on the
floor. On the back of the cave, there are a lot of bones piled up in a huge
mound.
name: Smelly Chamber

desc:
A small pile of half-eaten bones bare of flesh lies against the southern wall of
this burrow. In the pile, faeces and pieces of fur can be found. Dark and light
green moss lies all over the floor, serving as a soft mattress for the
inhabitants of the cave. Small bits of wet earth are found near the exit of the
burrow to the east, in which the pawprints of a bear-like creature lie.
name: Animal Burrow

desc:
The den looks fairly comfortable as it has been lined with leaves and grasses.
Outside the den grows a large amount of dense vegetation that hides the entrance
while still allowing some sunlight to enter. The walls of the den are made of
dirt, stone, and roots. A large boulder can be seen to the south.
name: Leaf-covered Bear Den

desc:
Sticky silk webs hang from the ceiling creating a sticky, milk-white curtain in
the tunnel. The passage bends towards the north from the east where the tunnel
rises slightly. Webs cover the sides of the muddy tunnel which are filled with
the cocoons and corpses of small mammals.
name: Webbed Tunnel

desc:
Thick, white webs cover the edges of the cavern and hang from the ceiling like
curtains of intricately-threaded gossamer strands. The bottom of the cave is
covered with mud, webbing, and a few discarded corpses which are completely
drained of fluid. A few chittering noises can be heard from the western wall.
name: Drafty Webbed Cavern

desc:
Hung in the corner of the cavern by thin, gossamer strands of silk is a thick,
white spider's nest. Countless bones and sucked-dry corpses litter the ground in
various states of decay. Several smaller passages, too small to enter, lead in
all directions where the movements of tiny arachnids' feet can be heard.
name: Spider's Nest

desc:
Sturdily built and spacious, this cellar was built by someone who knew what they
were doing, and wanted ample storage space. The ceiling and floor are of thick
wooden boards, while the walls are of hard-packed dirt. In some places, a
testimony to its age and state of decay, piles of dirt and small rocks litter
the cellar's floor. The ruins of shelves and a table also lie on the floor.
name: Old, Crumbling Cellar

desc:
All around is only the blackness of a deep, deep hole. Ground and air here do
not seem like substance at all, yet you can move and breathe. The air, or
whatever it is that is around, is freezing cold. It cannot be said for sure if
the dark becomes a bit darker or lighter in some places or if that impression is
just your imagination running wild.
name: Lost in the Freezing Dark

desc:
Stunted trees grow atop the rocky cliffs rising all around this secluded hollow.
The vegetation down here forms a tangle of vines, roots and fallen trees, partly
hiding the bottom of the dell from sight, yet leaving a few clear paths for
moving around. Apparently even large animals sometimes find their way here,
since the ground under one of the trunks has been dug with large paws and sharp
claws. A wet carpet of moss covers the soil here entirely, adding to the deep
green hue that colours the terrain. Dense shrubs block the paths out of the
hollow between the rocky outcroppings.
name: A Hidden Dell
